metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

evidence: 1 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief comment in `.github/workflows/codeql.yml` (or `.github/codeql/codeql-config.yml`) explaining the `docs/**` exclusion policy (rationale: unpackaged POCs only), and note it in AGENTS.md or CONTRIBUTING.md so future contributors know that code under `docs/` is not CodeQL-scanned.
evidence: Plan states: 'durable (survives future `docs/experiments` additions)' but provides no mechanism to warn future contributors about the exclusion's security implication.
No mention of updating AGENTS.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, or adding a comment in `codeql.yml` explaining the policy.
T5e documentation sub-check: 'A novel architectural decision is captured in an ADR / AGENTS.md / design-doc update.'
findings.finding: The `docs/**` CodeQL exclusion is a security-relevant scanning policy decision that is not surfaced in any developer-facing documentation (e.g., AGENTS.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, or a doc comment in the workflow). A future contributor adding code under `docs/` — including code that imports production modules — will have no indication that it is invisible to CodeQL, potentially masking real security findings.
scenarios: A contributor adds a utility script under `docs/helpers/` that imports `src/rebar` code and logs sensitive data; CodeQL never sees it because of the blanket exclusion.
A reviewer approving a PR that touches `docs/` does not realize the file is excluded from security scanning.
findings.suggested_fix:
In `.github/codeql/codeql-config.yml`, add a YAML comment:
```yaml
# docs/** is excluded because files there are unpackaged, hand-run POCs
# (never imported by src/rebar). If you add code under docs/ that imports
# production modules or handles sensitive data, consider moving it to src/.
paths-ignore:
  - "docs/**"
```
Also add one sentence to AGENTS.md (or CONTRIBUTING.md) under a CI/scanning section: 'Note: `docs/**` is excluded from CodeQL analysis; do not place security-sensitive production code there.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that `config-file:` is a valid input of `github/codeql-action/init@v4` by checking the action's [`action.yml`](https://github.com/github/codeql-action/blob/v4/init/action.yml) inputs section before merging.
evidence: Existing `.github/workflows/codeql.yml` init step (lines 42-44) only uses `languages:` — `config-file:` is not currently used anywhere in the repo, so this is an unobserved flag on the action.
Evidence class: Partially-verified — `config-file` is a broadly known CodeQL Action input, but it has not been directly confirmed against the action's `action.yml` for v4 specifically.
findings.finding: The plan depends on `config-file:` being a valid input of `github/codeql-action/init@v4`, but this specific flag has not been observed directly (the existing workflow only uses `languages:` on that step). This is a new parameter surface on an already-used action and has not been confirmed against the action's `action.yml` or live docs.
findings.location: What section / `.github/workflows/codeql.yml` init step (line 42-44) / Acceptance Criteria AC-2
scenarios: If `config-file:` input was renamed or removed in v4 of the action, the init step would silently ignore or error on the unknown input, meaning `docs/**` would never be excluded and the false-positive alerts would continue.
findings.suggested_fix: Browse `https://github.com/github/codeql-action/blob/v4/init/action.yml` and verify the `config-file` key exists under `inputs:`. If confirmed, add a comment in the workflow referencing the docs URL so future maintainers can re-verify on action upgrades.

findings.finding: The ticket has no `## Acceptance Criteria` checklist. A plan cannot be reviewed for completion without testable, checkable criteria.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an `## Acceptance Criteria` section with `- [ ]` checklist items, one per observable, in-session-verifiable outcome.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `-F` (fixed-string) to the AC2 grep proving command so dots in the path are matched literally, e.g. `grep -qF 'config-file: ./.github/codeql/codeql-config.yml' .github/workflows/codeql.yml`.
evidence: Taxonomy item (1): substring/regex metacharacter false-positive — dots in `./.github/codeql/codeql-config.yml` are unescaped regex wildcards when passed to grep without -F
The command is the sole proving command for AC2
findings.finding: The grep proving command in the Verification section uses `grep -q 'config-file: ./.github/codeql/codeql-config.yml'` without the `-F` (fixed-string) flag. The dots in the path are regex metacharacters that match any character, meaning the check could falsely pass against a subtly different string (e.g. `config-fileX__Xgithub`) and does not anchor the match to a full key-value line.
scenarios: A typo in the workflow file that replaces a dot with any character would still pass the grep check, giving a false green on AC2.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `grep -q` with `grep -qF` in both the Verification section and any inline AC text that repeats this command.
